data.body:
Research + design-of-record: provider-generic agent guidance (AGENTS.md migration)

CONTEXT. Session "provider generic guidance". Goal: slim the oversized CLAUDE.md, restrict
CLAUDE.local.md to machine/operator-local content, separate rebar's OWN dev guide from a
client-usable template, and generalize guidance + example skills across AI coding providers.

RESEARCH FINDINGS (verified against primary sources, July 2026):
- AGENTS.md is the de-facto cross-vendor standard for persistent project guidance. Created by
  OpenAI (Aug 2025); DONATED to the Linux Foundation's Agentic AI Foundation on 2025-12-09
  (co-founded by Anthropic, OpenAI, Block — alongside MCP and goose). ~60k+ repos. Un-specced:
  just Markdown named AGENTS.md at repo root; nearest-file-wins in monorepos; explicit chat
  prompts override. Anthropic co-founded the governing body but Claude Code does NOT yet read
  AGENTS.md (issue anthropics/claude-code#6235 open, unmet mid-2026).
- Native AGENTS.md readers (zero extra config): Codex, Cursor, Copilot CODING AGENT, Zed,
  Windsurf, Amp, Factory, Jules, Devin, RooCode, Cline.
- Gaps: Claude Code (reads CLAUDE.md only — bridge via one-line `@AGENTS.md` import, Windows-safe
  and leaves room for Claude-only notes); GitHub Copilot IN-EDITOR (.github/copilot-instructions.md);
  Cursor scoped .mdc rules (globs/alwaysApply — superset AGENTS.md can't express); Gemini CLI &
  Aider "listed" but don't auto-read AGENTS.md (need one config line each).
- Skills: Anthropic's SKILL.md (Agent Skills) is now an OPEN cross-provider format (Oct 2025),
  adopted by Codex, Copilot, Cursor, Gemini CLI, JetBrains Junie, etc. Emerging split:
  AGENTS.md=context, SKILL.md=workflows, MCP=tools. Format is portable; storage paths/triggers
  still differ per tool.
- OSS patterns (verified repos): symlink CLAUDE.md->AGENTS.md (apache/airflow,
  grafana/metrics-drilldown); thin `@AGENTS.md` pointer file (expo/expo,
  software-mansion/react-native-screens, cybercog/laravel-love); generator (dyoshikawa/rulesync);
  single AGENTS.md only. Consensus best practice: keep root file LEAN (sources cluster <200 lines,
  some <60) and push detail into linked/nested docs. Duplicating existing docs into the agent file
  is a named anti-pattern (raises agent cost ~23%).

REPO STATE (already ~30% migrated): no AGENTS.md/symlinks yet. CLAUDE.md=665 lines and duplicates
docs (gate protocols->plan-review-gate.md; MCP tools->mcp-reference.md; quality gates+ticket
template+project criteria->plan-review-criteria-guide.md; store-writes->concurrency.md;
git/Gerrit->CONTRIBUTING.md+docs). CLAUDE.md-NATIVE (no backing doc): idea status, session logs,
ticket hierarchy+parent-first cascade, linking, tags. Existing scaffolding: .agents/rules/rebar.md
(39-line neutral distillation), .agents/mcp_config.json, .codex/config.toml (fallback to CLAUDE.md
w/ comment "temporary ... until the guidance is migrated"), docs/session-id-shims.md (multi-provider),
examples/claude-skills/ (already reframed as retargetable; shared/test-design.md byte-synced by
tests/unit/test_skill_shared_sync.py). CLAUDE.local.md=155 lines; mixes machine/operator-local
(hourly main-tracking LaunchAgent; Gerrit identities RebarBotNava/JoeOakhartNava) with
generally-applicable rules duplicated from CLAUDE.md.

LOCKED DECISIONS (user, this session):
1. Root file: AGENTS.md canonical + thin CLAUDE.md (`@AGENTS.md` import) + de-dup bulk into docs.
2. Client template: a shipped `templates/AGENTS.md` kept in sync with docs by a test (mirrors the
   skill shared/ sync gate).
3. Next step: capture design as a rebar epic now; implementation follows as a reviewed feature branch.

KEY INSIGHT: "CLAUDE.md duplicates docs" and "separate rebar's guide from a client template" are the
SAME fix — push the portable "how to drive rebar" guidance into docs as the single source of truth;
have thin, audience-specific files (rebar's own AGENTS.md; the client template) point at it. rebar's
own AGENTS.md keeps only rebar-internal dev specifics (Gerrit split-remote, module-size, Serena,
local-dev-env, worktree/venv, CI-before-close, signing) inline + one-line pointers for the rest.
